hero.subtitle: Tbilisi's beloved open-air flea market by the river
hero.tip: Best on weekends — peak atmosphere & vendors

about.title: Why Visit Dry Bridge Market
about.description: Dry Bridge Market is Tbilisi's most iconic open-air bazaar, sprawling beneath the old Dry Bridge and along the leafy banks of the Mtkvari River. Since the early 1990s, locals have gathered here to sell everything from Soviet-era relics to hand-painted canvases, antique jewelry, vinyl records, and traditional Georgian crafts. It is a living museum of post-Soviet culture and Georgian creativity — chaotic, colorful, and endlessly fascinating.

highlights.0: Soviet memorabilia — military medals, vintage cameras, enamel pins, propaganda posters
highlights.1: Original oil paintings and watercolors by local Georgian artists
highlights.2: Antique jewelry, silver filigree, and cloisonné enamelwork
highlights.3: Vinyl records, old books, and hand-drawn maps of the Caucasus
highlights.4: Traditional Georgian handicrafts — drinking horns, felt hats, ceramics
highlights.5: Street photography paradise — every stall tells a story

reviews.title: Real Visitor Reviews
reviews.subtitle: Ratings and reviews from Google Maps (last updated: 2026). We only show a verified selection of highly-rated reviews.
reviews.seeMore: See more reviews on Google Maps →

0.name: David K.
0.text: Absolutely one of the best flea markets in the Caucasus. Spent hours browsing Soviet badges, old paintings, and quirky antiques. The vendors are friendly and love to share stories.

1.name: Nino T.
1.text: My favourite place in Tbilisi. Every weekend I find something new — old vinyl records, handmade jewelry. It's a treasure hunt every time.

2.name: Marco L.
2.text: A must-visit for anyone in Tbilisi. The atmosphere is incredible, especially on Saturdays. Great for unique souvenirs and street photography.

3.name: Lisa W.
3.text: Such a fascinating market! I found beautiful cloisonné enamel pieces and vintage Georgian postcards. Prices are reasonable if you bargain gently.

4.name: Sandro M.
4.text: This market is a living museum. War medals, old cameras, Soviet propaganda posters — the history on display here is remarkable.

5.name: Yuki S.
5.text: Came here on a Sunday morning. The bridge area was full of artists selling oil paintings. Bought a beautiful Tbilisi cityscape for my apartment.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how the Dry Bridge Market Administration Office ("we", "us", "our") collects, uses, and protects information when you visit drybridgemarket.com.

collection.title: Information We Collect
collection.text: This website does not require user registration or collect personal information directly. We may collect anonymised usage data (such as page views and browser type) through standard web analytics to improve site performance.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services — Google Maps
thirdParty.text: This website embeds Google Maps services and provides links to Google Maps for location, directions, and visitor reviews. When you interact with embedded Google Maps content or follow links to Google Maps, your data may be processed by Google LLC in accordance with Google's Privacy Policy (https://policies.google.com/privacy). This may include your IP address, device information, location data, and browsing behaviour. We do not control and are not responsible for Google's data processing practices. We recommend reviewing Google's Privacy Policy before interacting with embedded map content.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.text: This website uses essential cookies for language preference and theme (light/dark mode) selection. These cookies are strictly functional and do not track personal information. Third-party services embedded on this site (such as Google Maps) may set their own cookies. Please refer to our Cookie Settings page for details.

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: For privacy-related enquiries, please contact the Administration Office at +995 555 696 896.

cookies.title: Cookie Settings
cookies.intro: This page explains how drybridgemarket.com uses cookies and similar technologies.

essential.title: Essential Cookies
essential.text: We use strictly necessary cookies to store your language preference (English, ქართული, or 繁體中文) and your theme selection (light or dark mode). These cookies are required for the Website to function correctly and cannot be disabled.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies — Google Services
thirdParty.text: This Website embeds Google Maps, which may set cookies on your device when you view or interact with the embedded map. These cookies are managed by Google LLC and may be used for analytics, personalisation, and advertising purposes in accordance with Google's Cookie Policy (https://policies.google.com/technologies/cookies). The Dry Bridge Market Administration Office does not control these cookies. You may manage or block third-party cookies through your browser settings.

manage.title: Managing Your Cookie Preferences
manage.text: You can control cookies through your browser settings. Most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies. Please note that disabling essential cookies may affect the functionality of this Website (e.g., language and theme preferences may not be saved).

contact.title: Contact
contact.text: For questions about our use of cookies, please contact the Administration Office at +995 555 696 896.
